Off Calendar Sep-26-2017 Continued To Feb-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant Hilary Speck Ericson As Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF 2479 MARIN STREET, LLC's MOTION To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant Hilary Speck Ericson As Trustee, as follows: Plaintiff's motion to compel defendant's response to Special Interrogatory 11 is granted. Defendant's objections are overruled. The present market value of trust assets is discoverable because a probate proceeding or trust creditor claim proceeding was not filed after the death of the trustor. The trust beneficiaries have received distributions from the trust. Thus they are potentially exposed to personal liability to the extent the subject trust's debt cannot be satisfied from the trust estate. See Valentine v. Read, 50 Cal. App. 4th 787 (1996). Defendant's informal representation that sufficient trust funds exist to satisfy the debt is insufficient for formal discovery purposes. The information sought is necessary for plaintiff's trial preparation. All financial information provided shall be protected by a confidentiality order to be drafted by the moving party and approved by the defendant. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
